steps.title: Identify the research finding about age and borrowing
steps.content: The passage gives us a key finding: younger speakers of nonmajority languages borrow more from the majority language than older speakers do.

steps.title: Connect the research to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D states that younger Spanish speakers would be more likely to use the NYC-specific dialect than older speakers.

This follows directly from the research: younger speakers borrow more from the majority language (English), and the hypothesis claims the NYC dialect develops through English interaction.

A, B, and C either rely on unsupported neighborhood logic, predict general language growth, or contradict the research by focusing on borrowing from other nonmajority languages.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
